title: Goldfussia palawanensis
uses: Goldfussia palawanensis is a plant species native to the Philippines, but information about its specific medicinal uses is not readily available.
benefits: Goldfussia palawanensis is known to possess several health benefits due to its rich composition of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. intro: Goldfussia palawanensis, also known as 'Tawari', is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of the Philippines. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Goldfussia palawanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
